info.name: Municipal Law Skill for Autonomous Agents
info.description:
Infra/integration test suite for the Municipal Law Skill API. Targets the live Vercel deployment by default (see the {{base_url}} collection variable, or use the Vercel/Local environment files in postman/environments/). Grounded in real, verified behavior against the full ingested NYC Administrative Code + NYC Health Code corpus - not invented expected values.

Organized one folder per API (endpoint). Every request name is suffixed with 'Success Test' or 'Fail Test' so the Collection Runner summary tells you at a glance which category failed. Where a request has a JSON body, an extra '_test_note' field documents what that specific case validates - it's not a real API field; every request model in app/models.py uses pydantic's default extra='ignore', so the server silently drops it and it never affects the actual test. GET requests (no body) carry the same note in the request's own description field instead.

See postman/README.md for how to run this with Newman.

variable.description: Fill this in locally (or via the environment file) with the real INGEST_API_KEY value to exercise the authorized-ingest success path. Never commit a real value here - the collection ships with this blank on purpose.

item.name: 1. Service Info
GET / - Success Test: root service info reflects rebrand

request.description: Validates the service's root info endpoint reports the rebranded name and the expected links, and is not rate-limited.

exec: pm.expect(body.name).to.eql('Municipal Law Skill for Autonomous Agents');

item.name: GET /skill.md - Success Test: serves agent-facing reference as plain text

request.description: Validates /skill.md serves the repo's root SKILL.md live, as text/plain, with the rebranded title and without leaking internal-only endpoint details (e.g. /ingest) to agents.

exec: pm.expect(pm.response.text()).to.match(/^# Municipal Law Skill for Autonomous Agents/);
pm.test('does not document /ingest to agents', function () {

item.name: GET /api/v1/health - Success Test: reports live Mongo reachability

request.description: Validates the health check pings MongoDB and reports 'ok' when reachable. This service returns 200 either way (status:'degraded' if Mongo is unreachable), so this test only asserts the reachable/happy path.

item.name: GET /api/v1/version - Success Test: returns app version string

request.description: Validates /version returns a version field - used by the Rate Limiting folder as a cheap, side-effect-free endpoint to probe.

request.description: Validates the one-hop citation graph resolves 161.19's cross-reference to 161.01 within the ingested corpus, marking it resolved:true.

request.description: Validates an exact lookup for a section number that doesn't exist in the corpus returns 404 rather than an empty-but-200 response.

item.name: GET /sections/999.99/related - Fail Test: 404 for unknown section

request.description: Validates /related also 404s for a section number that doesn't exist, rather than returning an empty related list with a 200.

item.name: 6. Documents (chained from Search)
GET /documents/{{document_id}} - Success Test: metadata for a chained real document

request.description: Uses the document_id captured by the Search folder's first request. Validates document metadata (document_type, source_url) is returned for a real, existing document.

exec: console.warn('document_id is empty - run the "3. Search" folder first to populate it.');

item.name: GET /documents/{{document_id}}/chunks - Success Test: non-empty ordered chunk list

request.description: Validates every section belonging to the chained document is returned as a non-empty array.

exec: pm.test('returns a non-empty array of chunks', function () {

item.name: GET /documents/000000000000000000000000 - Fail Test: 404 for well-formed but nonexistent id

request.description: A syntactically valid 24-char hex ObjectId that (almost certainly) doesn't exist. Validates a clean 404 rather than a 500.

item.name: GET /documents/not-a-valid-objectid - Fail Test: 404 for malformed id

request.description: app/routers/documents.py::_object_id() catches bson.errors.InvalidId and raises 404 (not 500/422) for a malformed ObjectId string - validates that mapping.

exec: pm.test('status is 404 (malformed id, not a 500)', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(404); });

item.name: 7. Ingest (manual/optional - do not run in automated CI)
item.description: POST /ingest triggers real outbound fetches and Atlas writes, and is rate-limited to 1 request/minute in its own bucket. Run this folder manually and deliberately, not as part of an automated suite - it's excluded from any 'Run whole collection' workflow you should wire into CI. The 'Fail Test' requests here are always safe to run (they're rejected before any real ingestion happens); the 'Success Test' request only works if you've set the {{ingest_api_key}} collection variable to the real key locally.

item.name: No X-Ingest-Api-Key header - Fail Test: 401 unauthorized
Empty urls array - Fail Test: 422 validation error

exec: pm.test('status is 401 or 422 (either is an acceptable rejection)', function () {

exec: pm.test('status is 200 (authorized) or 401/429 (no key configured / already used this minute\'s quota)', function () {
pm.test('reports ok status for the URL', function () {

item.name: 8. Rate Limiting (stress test - consumes your 10-req/min budget)
item.description: Self-looping request that calls /api/v1/version 11 times in a row using postman.setNextRequest(), asserting the first 10 succeed (Success Test iterations) and the 11th returns 429 with a Retry-After header (Fail-path Test iteration, which is the *expected*/correct outcome). Run this folder in isolation (Collection Runner, this folder only) - running it alongside other requests in the same minute will eat into their rate-limit budget too, and running the whole collection back-to-back may trigger this before you intend to test it.

item.name: Rate Limit Probe - Success/Fail Test: loops itself 11x, expects 429 on the 11th

request.description: Iterations 1-10 are 'Success Test' (expect 200, still within budget); iteration 11 is the 'Fail Test' (expect 429 + Retry-After) that proves the limiter actually engages.

exec: pm.test('iteration 11/11 - Fail Test: rate limited (429)', function () {
pm.test('iteration 11/11 - Fail Test: Retry-After header present', function () {
postman.setNextRequest('Rate Limit Probe - Success/Fail Test: loops itself 11x, expects 429 on the 11th');
